Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to write a DuckDB SQL query that filters out cases that have a specific three event sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in direct succession and where the time between Create Order and Confirm Order in that sequence is more than 5 days Then return all events from cases that are NOT filtered out

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interpretation Direct succession means no other events in between for that case So we need to find cases where these three activities occur consecutively in order Then among those check if the duration between the second and third Create Order to Confirm Order exceeds 5 days Then exclude those cases entirely

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Approach Use window functions to identify the sequence For each case order events by timestamp Use LAG to get previous activity and next activity or use LEAD But we need to find triples Since direct succession we can check if an event is Create Order and its previous event is Approve Purchase Requisition and its next event is Confirm Order That gives us the middle event Then we can compute the time difference between that Create Order and its following Confirm Order If more than 5 days then that case is flagged

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we can use array agg or listagg to form a sequence and search for pattern but that is more complex Window functions are straightforward

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 For each case order by timestamp 2 Use LAG activity 1 OVER PARTITION BY case id ORDER BY timestamp as prev activity 3 Use LEAD activity 1 OVER PARTITION BY case id ORDER BY timestamp as next activity 4 Also use LEAD timestamp 1 to get next timestamp 5 Filter rows where activity equals Create Order AND prev activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition AND next activity equals Confirm Order 6 Compute duration equals next timestamp minus timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In DuckDB timestamp subtraction gives an interval Better to use interval comparison next timestamp minus timestamp greater than INTERVAL 5 days DuckDB supports interval literals

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then get distinct case ids that satisfy this condition Then select all events from event log where case id NOT IN those case ids

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Edge cases There could be multiple sequences in same case The condition says contains the following sequence so if any such sequence exists the whole case is excluded So we just need to detect at least one occurrence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Our window approach checks consecutive events However we need to ensure that the Approve Purchase Requisition is immediately before and Confirm Order immediately after That is correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Implementation Use CTE to get flagged case ids Then write the final SQL with a WITH clause
